headline: Cleveland Fed: AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may be needed
detail: Fed's Hammack warning that AI-driven demand could reignite inflation and force rate hikes is the single most relevant headline for your book today. High-multiple tech and semis are the most rate-sensitive names in the market - higher rates compress valuations fast. This directly threatens CRWD, SMH, SOXL, QQQ, and QQQ-linked holdings simultaneously.
rec: Consider trimming SOXL first - it's 3x levered semis, the most vulnerable name to a rate scare. No scanner long setup supports adding here, and margin amplifies the downside if yields spike today.

headline: Amazon investing $1B to follow Palantir's AI playbook
detail: Amazon doubling down on enterprise AI is a broad positive signal for the AI infrastructure theme - good for sentiment across your tech names. It doesn't directly move your specific holdings today but reinforces the long-term thesis behind CRWD and the chip ETFs.
rec: No action needed today - this supports the broader thesis but isn't a specific entry trigger for any holding, and the rate-hike headline offsets enthusiasm. Hold what you have.

headline: Blue Origin pivots launchpad after explosion, targeting end of 2026
detail: Blue Origin news is a mild negative for space-sector sentiment and indirectly relevant to SPCX given SpaceX competes in the same launch market. A competitor stumbling can be a slight relative positive for SpaceX, but SPCX is so new and thinly traded that any read-through is speculative.
rec: No trade action warranted - SPCX is a brand-new IPO with limited data and you're already in it. Just keep your manual risk management tight given its volatility and your margin exposure.

headline: Gold nearing 'death cross' that historically signals gains
detail: A gold technical pattern doesn't directly affect tech or semis, but if gold rallies it often signals risk-off or dollar weakness - both of which can be mixed signals for your concentrated tech book. Not an immediate threat but worth noting as a macro mood indicator.
rec: Nothing to do here for your portfolio - gold moves don't directly drive your names, and the Fed headline is a far bigger factor to track today.
